articles.title: Jamie Dimon says JPMorgan could do prediction markets — with big guardrails
articles.summary: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon told investors the bank could enter prediction markets if proper regulatory guardrails existed. The world's largest bank backing prediction markets would legitimize the industry overnight and potentially bring institutional money flooding in.

articles.title: As Prediction Markets Grow, Lawmakers and the Public Race to Keep Up
articles.summary: Prediction markets are expanding faster than regulators can write rules, creating a messy patchwork of oversight as lawmakers scramble to understand how these platforms actually work. The regulatory confusion creates both opportunity and risk for bettors, who benefit from current gaps but face potential crackdowns as politicians figure out whether they're looking at gambling, financial markets, or something entirely new.

articles.title: CFTC Top Cop Warns Against Insider Trading on Prediction Markets
articles.summary: The CFTC's enforcement director issued a warning about insider trading violations in prediction markets, signaling increased regulatory scrutiny of betting platforms. This puts prediction market operators and big bettors on notice that the same securities laws governing traditional markets now apply to political and event betting.

articles.title: wangpaifeixingyuan
articles.summary: Chinese space program officials are likely discussing or announcing new astronaut selection criteria or missions, given "wangpaifeixingyuan" roughly translates to "ace pilot astronaut" or "elite flying personnel." Space race developments create betting opportunities on mission timelines, crew selections, and geopolitical competition between China and other space powers.

articles.title: Prediction markets face DOJ scrutiny over well-timed bets on Nicolás Maduro capture, Iran strikes: sources
articles.summary: The Department of Justice is investigating suspiciously well-timed bets on prediction markets related to Nicolás Maduro's potential capture and Iranian military strikes, suggesting possible insider trading concerns. This scrutiny could trigger new regulatory crackdowns on prediction markets, particularly around geopolitical events where government officials might have advance knowledge.

articles.title: ‘Astonishing and Appalling’: Senator Blasts MLB-Polymarket Deal
articles.summary: A senator is furious about MLB's partnership with Polymarket, likely viewing the deal as legitimizing gambling on sports through prediction markets. The outrage signals potential regulatory pushback that could threaten similar partnerships between traditional sports leagues and betting platforms.

articles.title: How polling failures, gambling legalization and political gridlock paved the way for the explosive rise of prediction markets
articles.summary: Prediction markets exploded into mainstream consciousness because traditional polling became unreliable, sports betting went legal nationwide, and Washington gridlock made political outcomes feel like pure coin flips. The convergence created perfect conditions for Americans to abandon trust in experts and start putting real money behind their political hunches instead.

articles.title: As Utah battles Kalshi in court, Moore and Curtis look to regulate prediction markets in Congress
articles.summary: Utah is fighting Kalshi in court while Representatives Moore and Curtis push new congressional legislation to regulate prediction markets. The dual pressure from state litigation and federal lawmakers signals prediction markets are hitting a regulatory inflection point that could reshape which platforms survive and what Americans can bet on.

articles.title: Feds Sniffing Around Polymarket After Suspicious Bets
articles.summary: Federal investigators are examining unusual betting patterns on Polymarket that suggest possible market manipulation or insider trading. This probe could trigger the first major regulatory crackdown on prediction markets, potentially reshaping how these platforms operate in the US.

articles.title: At least three more Air Force members questioned on Iran war Polymarket betting
articles.summary: The Air Force is investigating at least three more service members for betting on Iran war markets on Polymarket, expanding beyond earlier cases. Military personnel having inside information on conflict decisions creates serious market integrity issues that could prompt regulators to restrict defense-related betting markets.

articles.title: Kalshi is aggressively advertising on TikTok, seemingly targeting young people while promising a “money hack”
articles.summary: Kalshi is flooding TikTok with ads that frame prediction markets as a "money hack" to young users who probably don't understand the risks. This aggressive youth marketing strategy could backfire spectacularly if regulators decide these ads cross the line into promoting gambling to minors.

articles.title: Polymarket trader exploits UFC blunder, turns $676 into $67,000 in under a minute
articles.summary: A Polymarket trader capitalized on UFC's incorrect announcement of a fight result, flipping $676 into $67,000 before the platform could correct its error. The incident highlights how traditional sportsbooks' operational mistakes create massive arbitrage opportunities for alert prediction market traders who can move faster than centralized betting platforms.
